methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Augury is a Series F robotics and manufacturing software company that has raised $75M to date. The valuation exercise draws on a broader Robotics & Manufacturing peer universe comprising one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers including Locus Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Bedrock Robotics.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach here. Using total raised ($75M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier associated with Series F financing rounds yields an implied valuation of $98M. This reflects the anticipated increase in company value between institutional financing rounds at this stage.

Peer-median triangulation is not feasible given data constraints. While 11 peers operate within the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, the narrow filters for same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers returned insufficient data points to establish a reliable median. Applying all-sector peers would introduce unwarranted noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $83M (low) to $112M (high) with a median of $98M. This range is anchored entirely to the stage-progression method, creating meaningful model uncertainty. Key unknowns include Augury's revenue run rate, growth profile relative to peers, and unit economics—none of which are captured in this dataset. Any valuation conclusion should be conditioned on deeper commercial and financial diligence.
